title: Asuka after Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Asuka after Instrumentality             Quote Post by Leighton » Fri Apr 22, 2011 12:38 pm I'm sure theres some topic or link to details on this topic but i really cbf finding it atm. SO, when Asuka is killed in episode 25', how does she re-embody at the end of 26'? I maybe misconceiving the concept of the HIP, but other members of Nerv who were killed, like Misato and Ritsuko couldn't return to human form right?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Apr 22, 2011 12:49 pm No, they could. Lets put it this way: In a human, there is the body, and the soul the embodies it. The the body is render inoperative (dead), the soul lives on, and goes to heaven/hell/whatever you want to do with it. This means that the soul lives on. Now, what instrumentality does (tell me if I'm wrong) is it takes all the souls and makes them into one being. But, souls can reject instrumentality, and return to the form they create. Now, you don't need a body to be part of instrumentality, only a soul. This means anything with a soul could become part of instrumentality. This also means that any soul can reject instrumentality, and return into the form they create. This means that, even though Asuka died, her soul remained, and it became part of Instrumentality. It rejected Instrumentality, and it embodied her former self (what Asuka looks like). This also means that Leonardo da Vinci, Archimedes, Martin Luther King, and George Washington could also reject Instrumentality and return to a form they create (which means that they could become squirrels if they wanted). Does this make sense?

[]:             Quote Post by Leighton » Fri Apr 22, 2011 1:03 pm Stryker wrote: No, they could. Lets put it this way: In a human, there is the body, and the soul the embodies it. The the body is render inoperative (dead), the soul lives on, and goes to heaven/hell/whatever you want to do with it. This means that the soul lives on. Now, what instrumentality does (tell me if I'm wrong) is it takes all the souls and makes them into one being. But, souls can reject instrumentality, and return to the form they create. Now, you don't need a body to be part of instrumentality, only a soul. This means anything with a soul could become part of instrumentality. This also means that any soul can reject instrumentality, and return into the form they create. This means that, even though Asuka died, her soul remained, and it became part of Instrumentality. It rejected Instrumentality, and it embodied her former self (what Asuka looks like). This also means that Leonardo da Vinci, Archimedes, Martin Luther King, and George Washington could also reject Instrumentality and return to a form they create (which means that they could become squirrels if they wanted). Does this make sense?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Apr 22, 2011 1:10 pm Leighton wrote: From what i can conclude now, pretty much no one had the heart to re-embody besides Shinji and Asuka - another annoying case..

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Apr 22, 2011 1:27 pm Azathoth wrote: We can't really conclude this from what we see. Maybe anyone with any sense reincarnated on the other side of the planet - the one that didn't have a giant decapitated god rotting on it, perhaps. Shinji and Asuka's solitude is intended to reflect their ongoing struggle with each other, not a state of the world for everyone else.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Fri Apr 22, 2011 2:08 pm What I don't really get is where the souls are going after the body dies. Is it the Chamber of Guf? Can any person who existed int he history of the world return from Third Impact and come back to life now?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Apr 22, 2011 2:34 pm rhfxz1s wrote: What I don't really get is where the souls are going after the body dies. Is it the Chamber of Guf? Can any person who existed int he history of the world return from Third Impact and come back to life now?

[]:             Quote Post by Rei Makes You Go Splat » Fri Apr 22, 2011 4:37 pm I always understood Evangelion's use of "soul" as to be more like essence. I don't think it necessarily points to some sort of afterlife or reincarnation, but rather more of an imprint of that person.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Apr 22, 2011 8:26 pm For the record, I don't think the dead can come back as their souls have probably moved on to...wherever souls go after the body dies. That doesn't hold true for the recently deceased (like Asuka and Misato) whose souls are still hanging around earth. I have absolutely no evidence to back this up, but the "everyone who's ever lived can come back!" just sounds like a crappy fanfic idea to me. So I guess its just a subjective opinion. Besides Asuka may have been a special case as a pilot or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Fri Apr 22, 2011 8:35 pm Do we actually know that she was killed?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Apr 22, 2011 8:38 pm She got impaled about ten times in the vital areas, dismembered and eaten all with a high sync ratio. Remember the arm that got split in half? She's dead.

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Fri Apr 22, 2011 9:27 pm perhaps, but never underestimate the human creature's ability to tank with massive defense/HP stats! *ahem apart from the fact that a videorecording (from inside the plug?) of a fourteen-year old girl torn apart by machines would make great antiseele propaganda to save Nerv's ass from raging zergmobs, the point of Asuka's death was to push Shinji into tehraeg mode and tang the world, so, if anything, she should probably be kept alive to remind him that the world died because he was too much of a wuss to face reality and do damage where he should have (on the MPEs) instead of making everybody sacrifice themselves for his sake. although i hoped she'd live somehow...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Apr 22, 2011 10:59 pm Stryker wrote: This also means that Leonardo da Vinci, Archimedes, Martin Luther King, and George Washington could also reject Instrumentality and return to a form they create (which means that they could become squirrels if they wanted).

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Apr 22, 2011 11:14 pm My longtime theory has been that due to her exceedingly high synch ratio, she converted into LCL (a la Shinjii in episode 19) the instant before the MP Evas struck the final blow, thereby saving her life and allowing her to be Instrumentalized. During the instant in which we see the arm of her plugsuit split in two, it's unclear whether her actual arm is still inside it, but it doesn't look like it. Not gonna get into the question of whether people who are already dead can be Instrumentalized because... that one, like so many others, is a black hole, with contradictory evidence all over the place. However, it's worth pointing out that if the dead - even the recently dead - can't be Instrumentalized, Rei could still have converted Misato as she was in the process of drawing her last breath and before/during the detonation. Both 25 and 26' suggest that Misato has been Instrumentalized... Ritsuko, however, is another question, and one that (of course ) doesn't have an answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Apr 23, 2011 2:38 am You need to remember that EVA-02's remains stayed on the ground for that huge explosion as the Geofront was ripped free. What happened to Misato is probably the best guide to Asuka's fate -- it's just that we are not shown whether she just got a generic school uniform Rei or something personalized.

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Sat Apr 23, 2011 9:22 am gatotsu911 wrote: My longtime theory has been that due to her exceedingly high synch ratio, she converted into LCL (a la Shinjii in episode 19) the instant before the MP Evas struck the final blow, thereby saving her life and allowing her to be Instrumentalized. During the instant in which we see the arm of her plugsuit split in two, it's unclear whether her actual arm is still inside it, but it doesn't look like it.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Mon Apr 25, 2011 4:13 pm gatotsu911: Wait, so why can't dead people be Instrumentalized? I've always thought that they could. Kaji was clearly dead, and he appeared in Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Miuzaki » Mon Apr 25, 2011 4:21 pm AshPhoenix wrote: gatotsu911: Wait, so why can't dead people be Instrumentalized? I've always thought that they could. Kaji was clearly dead, and he appeared in Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Knuckles » Mon Apr 25, 2011 4:23 pm There's a certain time window that determines how far back deceased people are able to be sucked into Instrumentality. No one knows how far back that is but the writers/Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by Miuzaki » Mon Apr 25, 2011 4:33 pm Knuckles wrote: There's a certain time window that determines how far back deceased people are able to be sucked into Instrumentality. No one knows how far back that is but the writers/Anno.

I maybe misconceiving the concept of the HIP, but other members of Nerv who were killed, like Misato and Ritsuko couldn't return to human form right?
